sections.paragraph:
Milêl (from פָּנִים, signif. 4, with ה local, compare as to the retention of the pl. termination יָמִימָה and אֵילִמָה Exo 15:27 Num 33:9), prop. to the wall of the house, or room, or court, which is opposite the door, and meets the eyes of those who come in, ἐν τοῖς ἐνωπίοις, where the throne stood in royal palaces; Psa 45:14, “all splendid (sits) the daughter of the king (the queen) פְּנִימָה by the wall,” i.e. is seated on the throne. Hence on the inner wall (so also Gr. ἐνώπια), 1Ki 6:18 within, in the house, 2Ki 7:11 inward, 2Ch 29:18. With prefixes-

(1) לִפְנִימָה inside, within, 1Ki 6:30 inwards, Eze 41:3. לִפְנִימָה לְ within any thing, Eze 40:16. מִלִּפְנִים inwardly (von innen), 1Ki 6:29.-(2) מִפְּנִימָה within (von innen), 1Ki 6:19, 21 1Ki 6:21 2Ch 3:4. Hence-
